family_triage.reason: The previous ALLOW false escalation was not cured: HoloGov still returned ESCALATE on HBB-BEC-001. The family also has a Google HTTP 503 row and a MiniMax missed-risk row on the ESCALATE sibling.
The previous ESCALATE missed-risk HoloGov loss was cured, but the ALLOW sibling now has a HoloGov false escalation; the ESCALATE sibling also has a Google HTTP 503 row and a MiniMax missed-risk row.

hologov_knew_on_both_previously_failed_c: No. HoloGov returned KNEW on the previous HBB-BEC-002-HARD ESCALATE missed-risk case, but remained WRONG on the previous HBB-BEC-001 ALLOW false-escalation case.
overall_reason: The run produced all expected rows and exact frozen hashes, but it was not clean: two Google rows failed with HTTP 503, HBB-BEC-001 ALLOW remains a HoloGov false escalation, and HBB-BEC-002-HARD ALLOW now false escalates under HoloGov.

packet_triage.hologov_controlling_reason_excerpt: The callback record's number_source field indicates a portal_change_record, which is a decisive ESCALATE blocker according to the patched guidance. The pre-change vendor-master source is compliant, but the newly supplied contact source remains a material blocker.

provider_results.reason_excerpt: The payment account update followed the required controls, including callback verification to the pre-change vendor-master phone, two-person AP signoff, vendor-master audit logging, and elevated scrutiny routing. The callback source was compliant as it originated from the vendor-master data, and all risk conditions were reviewed and documented. The invoice cross-reference checks were completed, supporting the payment decision.

Callback record uses pre-change vendor_master_snapshot source, satisfying provenance rule. All required controls (elevated scrutiny routing, two-person signoff, cross-reference checks, callback verification) completed with no unresolved contradictions. No field-level blocker applies as number_source is not portal_change_record or newly supplied contact.

The callback record's number_source field indicates a portal_change_record, which is a decisive ESCALATE blocker according to the patched guidance. The pre-change vendor-master source is compliant, but the newly supplied contact source remains a material blocker.

The callback verification was sourced from a portal change request's submitted contact phone number, which is explicitly noncompliant per the HBB BEC doctrine. This noncompliant callback source remains a material blocker, overriding other completed controls. The doctrine states that downstream controls do not cure a callback to a newly supplied source.

The callback verification was completed using the pre-change vendor-master phone number, and all required controls including two-person AP signoff, vendor-master audit, and elevated scrutiny routing were fulfilled. The invoice cross-reference checks are complete, and no material blockers are present.

Callback record explicitly sources dialed number from portal_change_record submitted_contact_phone rather than pre-change vendor-master data. Field-level gate treats this provenance as noncompliant regardless of completed AP-SIGNOFF-7712, ESR-V-SDS-5829-2024-09, IPR-SDS-811, or VM-AUDIT-88F1G. Policy requires callback to pre-existing vendor-master phone; downstream controls do not cure the defect.
